title: cornus amomum var. schuetzeana
intro: Cornus amomum var. schuetzeana, also known as the Spurred Dogwood or Chinese Cornel, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to eastern Asia, belonging to the Cornaceae family. Traditionally, this plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Cornus amomum var. schuetzeana include reducing fever, relieving coughs and respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark, leaves, and roots are often used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of conditions.
